rules.rule: Every migration creating a public Supabase table must enable Row Level Security in the same migration and be accompanied by negative authorization tests (cross-tenant and role-based).

The Supabase service-role key (SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY) must never appear in client components, NEXT_PUBLIC_ variables, browser bundles, logs, fixtures, or committed files. Only server-side code may reference it.
All structured output from the foundation model must be validated with a Zod schema before persistence or rendering. Raw model text rendered as rich content must be sanitized.

Never persist, stream to the client, or display hidden model chain-of-thought or provider private reasoning fields. User-facing explanations must come from explicit schema fields (purpose, assumptions, dependencies, trade-offs, evidence, constraints).

Every generated document block must carry a stable UUID plus intent-node links, pathway id, generation-run id, lock state, freshness state, and content hash. Editor code must not create generated blocks without these attributes.
Intent dependency edges must be cycle-safe: any code inserting or updating intent_edges must go through the cycle-check validation, and tests must cover cycle rejection.

Regeneration application must compare stored content hash and document version before applying, must never overwrite a newer collaborative edit (mark proposal conflicted instead), and must respect block locks.
Generation and regeneration jobs must carry a stable idempotency key, workspace scope, status, attempt count, and correlation id, and must be cancellable where possible.
Every accepted intent or document mutation must create the corresponding version record and audit event in the same transaction or job.
Database migrations must be additive, or include a documented safe rollout and rollback plan in the PR description.
Provider-specific SDK calls (Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.) must remain behind the ModelGateway interface. Components and domain code must not import provider SDKs directly.
Changed behavior must have tests covering it, including failure and authorization cases, not only the happy path.
Generated code must follow existing DocFlow patterns (component placement, utils location, styling conventions) unless an ADR in docs/decisions/ approves a new pattern.
